summary: First serious live multi-slice pilot under the serious_live_multi_slice_no_remediation standard. Three bounded live Claude Code slices (status, tests, docs) executed through claude -p runtime launches with a 2.00 USD budget cap each. All three slices completed; each used exactly one live retry (within the max-1 rule). Attempt failures and causes, recorded honestly: status attempt 1 exceeded the budget because the initial launch plans carried the default Read/Write tool list without Edit, forcing a full-file rewrite; tests attempt 1 self-blocked cleanly with written evidence (tooling_budget_constraint) instead of risking a mid-write budget death, and its retry applied the exact two-assertion plan from the blocked attempt; docs attempt 1 hit the operator plan session limit after the deliverable was already written, and the retry confirmed without duplication. The only coordinator interventions were launch-plan configuration (regenerating tests/docs plans with -AllowedTool Edit,Read,Write), preflight parameterization, and executor-packet metadata corrections; zero coordinator-authored changes to slice product files, verifiable by diff. Runtime summaries: 3 of 3 ready_for_policy_gates, 0 blocked, autonomy metrics policy_gate_ready_rate 1.
policy_result: Compliant. Live execution used bounded claude -p with budget cap 2.00 USD per launch, ASCII-bounded redacted diagnostics, Read/Write/Edit tools only. No production, secrets, billing, auth, authorization, database, dependency installation, deployment, or CI/CD security changes. Issue #194 untouched and still excluded as not_planned by the operator decision registry.
risk_result: Medium. Live runtime launches consume operator plan budget and are subject to plan session limits, which caused one environmental retry. No remediation risk materialized: the coordinator made zero edits to slice product files.
unresolved_risks: prepare-runtime-launch defaults AllowedTool to Read,Write without Edit, which forces full-file rewrites and burned one slice budget; recommend changing the default in a runner-hardening task.
Operator plan session limits can fail live slices for reasons outside the protocol; retry timing depends on the reset window.
Slice retries consumed up to double the per-slice budget envelope (5 live executions total against a 3-execution baseline).
merge_status: PR pending. Squash merge after Foundation Validation, review gate, PR review report and Claude review pass.
